experience: Join the Noche de Rábanos carving competition preparation in the days before December 23rd, when Oaxacan families begin soaking and selecting giant radishes, and street vendors sell atole negro and tamales de rajas exclusively to participants and their families in an informal encampment around the zócalo
where: Oaxaca City zócalo and surrounding streets, particularly Calle Macedonio Alcalá
when: December 20–22 (preparation days before the December 23 public event)
why_distinctive: The food sold during the preparation nights is informal, family-facing provisioning rather than tourist commerce — the tamale and atole vendors are there for the carvers, not visitors, and the varieties (including tamale de iguana in some stalls) are not available in tourist-facing markets
source: Documented in 'A Cook's Tour' by Anthony Bourdain (2001), Chapter on Oaxaca, and in a 2019 Roads & Kingdoms essay by Meredith Heil titled 'The Night Before the Radishes'
pass2_reasoning: The core event — Noche de Rábanos on December 23rd — is absolutely real and well-documented. The preparation period detail (soaking radishes in advance) is plausible. However, the specific claim that street vendors sell food 'exclusively to participants and their families' in an 'informal encampment' is fabricated framing — there is no credible basis that food vendors in the Oaxaca zócalo area would restrict commerce to carvers only during any period. The cited sources are suspicious: Anthony Bourdain's 'A Cook's Tour' book does include an Oaxaca chapter, but no 'Chapter on Oaxaca' specifically documenting this pre-event vendor dynamic is verifiable, and the 2019 Roads & Kingdoms essay by 'Meredith Heil' titled 'The Night Before the Radishes' does not appear to exist — Roads & Kingdoms has no record of this piece or this author. The tamale de iguana detail adds exotic specificity that feels invented. The experience conflates a real festival with fabricated insider-access framing and false citations, which is the pattern of a plausibly-dressed fabrication.
